title: Prompt & Prefix Tuning
learning_outcomes: Compare virtual token prefix insertion with LoRA.

content_sections.title: Prompt Tuning and Prefix Tuning
content_sections.content:
Prompt Tuning and Prefix Tuning are early parameter-efficient fine-tuning architectures:

*   **Prompt Tuning:** Prepends $K$ trainable continuous embedding vectors ('virtual tokens') directly to the input sequence embeddings: $\mathbf{X}_{tuned} = [\mathbf{P}_{embed}; \mathbf{X}]$. Only these prefix tokens are updated during training. While simple, prompt tuning struggles to converge for models under 10 billion parameters.
*   **Prefix Tuning:** Prepends trainable key ($\mathbf{P}_K$) and value ($\mathbf{P}_V$) activations to the attention matrices at every transformer layer, modifying the attention calculation directly:

$$\text{Attention}(\mathbf{Q}, [\mathbf{P}_K; \mathbf{K}], [\mathbf{P}_V; \mathbf{V}])$$

This provides more stable training convergence across various model scales.
